summary: Large decomp-matching pass that replaced many `/// #` placeholders for simple proxy/trampoline functions with C bodies that only call one other function. The work was found with a new `table-typer dups` command, which grouped duplicated asm bodies and identified unmatched functions sharing the same opcode sequence as known proxy functions. The decomp.dev bot reported 114 new matches and +3648 matched bytes. Most changes landed in Kirby copy-ability motion callbacks, with smaller proxy implementations in ftCommon, Popo, stage files, menu, and toy code. The author deliberately left 8 proxy functions unimplemented because they lacked placeholders; follow-up comment clarified those TUs likely had not been scaffolded yet.

key_files.role: Main implementation batch; many Kirby motion-state proxy callbacks now directly call existing shared physics, collision, animation, walk, jump, and model-scale helpers.
Added prototypes for newly implemented unnamed Kirby proxy functions such as `fn_800FE0E0` and `fn_8010B1D4`.
Added and expanded tooling to locate duplicate function bodies and opcode-sequence candidates, including the new `dups` subcommand used to find the proxy backlog.
Implemented several common fighter proxy functions, including ItemScope phys and CaptureWait low callbacks.
Implemented Popo special-move physics proxy callbacks.
Added declarations for newly implemented Popo proxy callbacks.
Implemented a stage proxy function forwarding to an existing Corneria helper.
Implemented stage proxy function(s); exact callees are not fully visible in the excerpt.
Implemented stage proxy function(s) and adjusted related header declaration.
Implemented stage proxy function(s) and adjusted related header declaration.
Implemented stage proxy function(s) and adjusted related header declaration.
Implemented menu proxy function and adjusted related header declaration.

what_changed: Replaced many `/// #function` placeholders with one-call C functions of the form `void name(Fighter_GObj* gobj) { callee(gobj); }` or equivalent one-argument proxy signatures.

In `ftCo_Attack100.c`, implemented proxies such as `ftCo_ItemScopeEnd_Phys -> ftCo_800CDE54`, `ftCo_ItemScopeAirEnd_Phys -> ftCo_800CDE74`, `ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Anim -> ftCo_CaptureWaitHi_Anim`, and `ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_IASA -> fn_800DC014`.

In `ftKb_Init.c`, implemented many Kirby ground/air physics proxies using recurring callees: ground-state callbacks frequently call `ft_80084F3C`, air-state callbacks frequently call `ft_80084EEC` or `ft_80084DB0`, and selected walk/jump/collision callbacks forward to helpers such as `ftWalkCommon_800DFDDC`, `ftWalkCommon_800E0060`, `ftCo_KneeBend_Check_ShortHop`, `ftCo_Jump_Phys_Inner`, `ft_80083F88`, and `ftCo_AirCatchHit_Coll`.
Added missing includes in `ftKb_Init.c` for helpers used by newly implemented proxies: `ftCo_Jump.h`, `ftCo_KneeBend.h`, and `ftwalkcommon.h`.
Added header prototypes for newly exposed proxy implementations in Kirby and Popo headers.
Implemented smaller proxy functions across several stage/menu/toy TUs; one visible example is `grCorneria_801DFBCC` forwarding to `grCorneria_801DED50`.

Expanded `tools/table-typer` with root auto-location, updated report loading via `ninja`, enhanced `opseq` behavior, source placeholder/function definition location output, and a new `dups` command that finds duplicated asm function bodies.

Left 8 identified proxy functions unimplemented because they lacked `/// #` placeholders, including `fn_80246F0C`, `fn_802514B8`, several `un_*_OnFrame` wrappers, and related `vi_8031CAAC`/`HSD_*` callees.
smart_moves: Used duplicated opcode sequences rather than manual browsing to find a broad class of trivial unmatched functions.
Anchored the search on a known matched proxy (`ftCl_AppealS_IASA`, which simply calls `ftCo_AppealS_IASA`) to identify functions with the same proxy-shaped asm body.
Generated proxy C by parsing assembly for the callee, then looking up the callee's type signature, reducing risk of wrong argument or return types.
Skipped functions with no `/// #` placeholder instead of guessing their location, preserving TU ownership and avoiding potentially misplaced implementations.
Added the `table-typer dups` command so the duplicate-body discovery technique is reusable for future mass matching, not just a one-off script.
Kept proxy bodies minimal and idiomatic: no extra locals or control flow, just direct forwarding to the known callee, which is the correct tactic for these 32-byte wrappers.
decomp_lessons: Simple one-call proxy functions can be found at scale by grouping normalized asm bodies by opcode sequence and comparing matched references with unmatched candidates.
For wrappers, matching usually depends on using the exact existing callee and the correct function signature; a generated body is safer when it derives the signature from already declared code.
Do not implement functions in a TU merely because asm reveals a callee; if no placeholder exists, the TU may not have been scaffolded and file placement is not yet established.

A recurring fighter convention is that many ground physics callbacks forward to shared ground physics helpers while corresponding air callbacks forward to shared air physics helpers; this pattern can guide but should not replace asm/callee verification.
Mass proxy matching benefits from updating headers at the same time as C bodies when newly implemented functions are referenced externally or belong in existing declaration tables.
Tooling that reports both matched duplicates and unmatched duplicate candidates can convert repetitive decomp work into a searchable backlog sorted by match-byte impact.
naming_conventions: `ftKb_*_Phys`, `ftKb_*_Anim`, `ftKb_*_IASA`, and `ftKb_*_Coll` names preserve motion-state callback suffix conventions while proxying to shared helper implementations.
Kirby copy-ability names use compact character prefixes in the existing style, e.g. `Mr`, `Lg`, `Pk`, `Kp`, `Lk`, `Ss`, `Fx`, `Ns`, `Dk`, `Sk`, `Mt`, `Pp`, and `Ms` within `ftKb_*` symbols.
Unknown proxy wrappers remain named `fn_800FE0E0`, `fn_8010B1D4`, etc. where no semantic name was introduced by the PR.
Header declarations retain address comments such as `/* 0FE0E0 */ void fn_800FE0E0(Fighter_GObj* gobj);`.
Stage proxy names retain module-prefixed address-style names like `grCorneria_801DFBCC` rather than inventing semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: New `table-typer dups` normalizes asm function bodies by stripping labels and operands down to opcode names, then groups identical bodies to find duplicate functions.
`dups` separates matched reference functions from unmatched candidate functions using `build/GALE01/report.json`, allowing known implementations to serve as templates.
`opseq` was enhanced to accept opcode sequences from a file or comma-separated argument and to optionally list non-matching candidates with `-candidates`.
The updated opcode matching supports wildcard-like operands (`_`) and consistent operand variables, improving searches beyond literal line containment.
`loadReport` now runs `ninja -C <root>` before reading `build/GALE01/report.json`, ensuring table-typer decisions are based on the current match report.

The author used a generated-script workflow: identify duplicate proxy candidates with `table-typer dups`, parse asm to extract each callee, look up callee signature, and emit the one-call C implementation.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported GALE01 matched code increased to 45.36%, a +0.09% improvement and +3648 bytes, with 114 new matched functions.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.summary: Clarified that missing `/// #` placeholders usually mean `scaffold.py` has not been run on that TU, generally because nobody has worked on it yet.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining proxy functions without `/// #` placeholders after running `scaffold.py` on untouched TUs.
Run `tools/table-typer dups` with no target to rank duplicate unmatched bodies by potential matched bytes.
For a specific unmatched function, run `tools/table-typer dups <function>` to find matched proxy templates or sibling candidates.
Search for remaining placeholders whose asm body is a single call and return.
Audit newly scaffolded `un` or HSD-related TUs for the 8 proxies listed in the PR body.
